since_last: 36% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +2.31%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 36% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.13% · BTC -2.71% · Gold +1.61% · Nasdaq100 +0.81%. 36% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +2.31%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +2.31%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -2.77%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +15.96%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +15.96%, AMAT +10.11%, TER +8.75%, KLAC +7.41%, QCOM +5.88%. Laggards: INOD -10.91%, DELL -6.07%, AI -5.42%, OKLO -5.06%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.30% at $152.54
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.30% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
